fundamentals.name: Bloom Energy Corporation
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Bloom Energy Corporation designs, manufactures, sells, and installs solid oxide fuel cell systems for on-site power generation in the United States and internationally. It offers Bloom Energy Server, an energy server platform to convert fuel, such as natural gas, biogas, hydrogen, or a blend of these fuels, into electricity through a non-combustion electrochemical process. The company also provides Bloom Electrolyzer for producing hydrogen. It sells its products through direct and indirect sales channels to utilities, data centers, retail, healthcare, education, telecom, manufacturing, and other industries. The company was formerly known as Ion America Corp. and changed its name to Bloom Energy Corporation in 2006. Bloom Energy Corporation was incorporated in 2001 and is headquartered in San Jose, California.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue and strong recent earnings surprise are offset by a high valuation, significant debt, and low profitability margins.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 242.4%; next earnings date is 2026-04-28.
positives: Revenue growth 130.4% YoY — exceptional top-line expansion.
Last quarter EPS surprise 242.4% — significant beat on earnings.
Analyst growth estimates are very high for current Q (286.1%) and FY (179.8%).
Strong current ratio (5.03) and quick ratio (3.98) indicate good short-term liquidity.
negatives: Forward P/E of 69.97x and Price/Sales of 35.13x indicate a very high valuation.
Profit margin is only 0.2%, suggesting very thin profitability.
Return on equity is low at 1.3%.
Debt/Equity ratio is high at 311.48%, indicating significant leverage.
Mean analyst target of $260.18 is below the current spot price.

news.paragraph: Bloom Energy (BE) received an upgrade to Outperform from Daiwa, following recent positive mentions in industry analyses highlighting its outperformance over the past six months. The company also recently reported a significant EPS beat for its last quarter, exceeding estimates by over 240%. With strong analyst growth estimates for the current quarter and fiscal year, and a consensus 'buy' rating from 24 analysts, the news flow is decidedly bullish.
